question: Q.1 A person who does not believe in the existence of God.
explanation: Atheist (a=without + theo=god) is someone who lacks belief in God. Theist believes in God; Fatalist believes in fate; Optimist looks at the bright side.

question: Q.2 Handwriting which is difficult or impossible to read.
explanation: Illegible refers to bad handwriting. Inaudible cannot be heard; Inedible cannot be eaten; Inevitable cannot be avoided.

question: Q.3 A speech delivered without any previous preparation.
explanation: Extempore is a speech given without planning. Maiden is a first-time speech; Debate is an argument; Lecture is a prepared talk.

question: Q.4 A person who is unable to pay his debts.
explanation: Insolvent is someone who has no money to pay debts. Solvent can pay debts; Mercenary works only for money; Egoist is self-centered.

question: Q.5 One who loves mankind and works for their welfare.
explanation: Philanthropist (phil=love + anthropos=human) helps others. Misanthrope hates humans; Atheist denies God; Fastidious is hard to please.

question: Q.6 One who is present everywhere.
explanation: Omnipresent (omni=all) is being everywhere at once. Omniscient knows all; Omnipotent is all-powerful; Optimist is positive.

question: Q.7 Something that is certain to happen and cannot be avoided.
explanation: Inevitable is something that must happen. Invisible cannot be seen; Infallible never fails; Inaudible cannot be heard.

question: Q.8 One who is very difficult to please.
explanation: Fastidious is very concerned with accuracy and detail. Gullible is easily fooled; Egoist thinks only of self; Fatalist believes in fate.

question: Q.9 A person who has reached the age of 100 years.
explanation: Centenarian (cent=100) is 100 years old. Octogenarian is 80; Septuagenarian is 70; Veteran is someone with long experience.

question: Q.10 One who eats human flesh.
explanation: Cannibal eats its own species (humans). Herbivorous eats plants; Carnivorous eats meat; Omnivorous eats both.

question: Q.11 A post held without receiving any salary.
explanation: Honorary is a title or role without pay. Voluntary is by choice; Mercenary is for money only; Charity is giving to the needy.

question: Q.12 A person who looks at the dark side of things.
explanation: Pessimist expects the worst. Optimist expects the best; Theist believes in God; Egoist is self-absorbed.

question: Q.13 One who knows everything.
explanation: Omniscient (omni=all + scientia=knowledge) is all-knowing. Omnipotent is all-powerful; Omnipresent is everywhere; Scholar is a learned person.

question: Q.14 A person who leaves his own country to settle in another.
explanation: Emigrant (e=exit) leaves a country. Immigrant (i=in) enters a country; Tourist is a visitor; Nomad has no fixed home.

question: Q.15 Something which catches fire easily.
explanation: Inflammable (or flammable) burns easily. Inedible is not for eating; Inaudible cannot be heard; Invisible cannot be seen.

question: Q.16 One who is easily deceived or tricked.
explanation: Gullible is easily persuaded to believe something. Fastidious is picky; Optimist/Pessimist refer to outlook on life.

question: Q.17 Persons living at the same time.
explanation: Contemporaries live during the same period. Ancestors are from the past; Colleagues work together; Peerage refers to nobility.

question: Q.18 One who does something only for money.
explanation: Mercenary is motivated only by profit. Volunteer works for free; Philanthropist donates; Egoist is selfish.

question: Q.19 A sound that cannot be heard.
explanation: Inaudible (in=not + audire=hear) is too quiet to hear. Infallible makes no mistakes; Illegible is unreadable; Invisible is unseeable.

question: Q.20 A person who is all-powerful.
explanation: Omnipotent (omni=all + potens=power) has unlimited power. Omniscient knows all; Omnipresent is everywhere; Mighty is just strong.

question: Q.21 One who hates mankind.
explanation: Misanthrope (miso=hate) dislikes people. Philanthropist loves people; Optimist is positive; Fatalist believes in destiny.

question: Q.22 A person who believes that all events are predetermined by fate.
explanation: Fatalist believes everything is fated. Theist/Atheist refer to God; Pessimist is a negative thinker.

question: Q.23 Something that cannot be seen.
explanation: Invisible is not visible to the eye. Inaudible relates to hearing; Inedible to eating; Illegible to reading.

question: Q.24 One who believes in God.
explanation: Theist (theo=god) believes in God. Atheist does not; Pessimist is negative; Fatalist believes in fate.

question: Q.25 A person who thinks only of himself.
explanation: Egoist is self-centered. Altruist is selfless; Optimist is positive; Gullible is easily tricked.

question: Q.26 Something not fit to be eaten.
explanation: Inedible is not safe or good to eat. Delicious is tasty; Inaudible is quiet; Invisible is unseeable.

question: Q.27 A person who regards the whole world as his country.
explanation: Cosmopolitan is a 'citizen of the world'. Patriot/Nationalist love their specific country; Citizen is a legal member of a state.

question: Q.28 One who never makes a mistake.
explanation: Infallible (in=not + fallere=deceive/fail) is incapable of error. Inaudible/Invisible/Inevitable relate to sound/sight/fate.

question: Q.29 A person who comes to a foreign country to settle there.
explanation: Immigrant moves INTO a new country. Emigrant moves OUT OF their country; Tourist is a visitor; Expat lives abroad.

question: Q.30 A person who looks at the bright side of things.
explanation: Optimist is hopeful and confident. Pessimist is the opposite; Theist/Atheist refer to religious belief.
